dimensions.note: Report-only extension; it shapes collected workflow data and makes no AWS calls. No credentials or secrets introduced. The newly imported _lib/scan_error.ts twin is pure (zod + functions, SDK-free), so the report bundle stays SDK-free.

No logging calls changed. The existing collect() warn-on-malformed-row logging (with structured field paths) still fires; a network-kind row now decodes rather than warning, which is the intended strict-widen behavior.

The single degraded path is unchanged and still resets every derived source together. The scan-error decode now uses the canonical ScanErrorSchema (via the twin), so a `network`-kind row with the `service` tag parses into errors instead of failing safeParse and being miscounted into skipped — verified by a new synthetic-network-row decode test (counted, not skipped, rendered).

A new collect/report test decodes a synthetic `kind:'network', service:'rds'` row and asserts it is counted (errorsByKind.network===1), not skipped, and rendered in the transient-network section with its service/region pair. The degraded-payload reset test gains network:0 in its errorsByKind assertion. The producer-import drift tripwire (ModelScanError imported from the producer entrypoint) still guards schema agreement; its scanErrorFixture/no_regions fixtures gain the required `service` field.
Report-only extension; it renders collected workflow data and creates/updates/deletes no external resources, so there is no idempotency surface to assess.
No direct external API client; the report consumes swamp data/artifacts only. No request/response contract to verify.
No new files, handles, or network clients. The added import is a pure twin module; collection still reads artifacts via dataRepository.getContent and decodes in-memory.

Manifest version bumped 2026.06.22.0 -> 2026.06.26.1 (a report carries no version field; the metadata test keys off name/scope/labels). README errorsByKind line updated to list the new `network` bucket and note network rows render in their own section, not folded into `other`. No real infra identifiers added; fixtures use fictional placeholders.
